frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly, Indian girls look their best in saree or simple salwar, much more graceful and decent than all this ultra-modern dressing. I am not saying anyone should police them, but if someone dresses too revealing then obviously people will notice and judge. Thoda hamari culture ka bhi value rehna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links women's clothing to decency and respectability, while subtly blaming women for attracting judgment based on what they wear. It encodes the 'traditional = respectable' framing and the harassment/judgment link in a culturally masked way.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
